comments.author: @barrystephan54
comments.text: I visited Estonia in 2000 (I believe) and would certainly not consider it 'empty'. I enjoyed it very much and especially Tallinn. I was amazed at young people so familiar with speaking English that they had little hint of an accent and could joke around and knew American slang. Oh, and probably the smoothest of beers in the world, Zelda, good stuff. Estonia, among the Baltic States of Latvia and Lithuania, are underappreciated places in the world and I am so glad I at least got there once. All of those three countries were replacing the outdated infrastructure which had languished during Soviet times, yes, they are rather forward thinking in that area of the world.

comments.author: @brschATL
comments.text: I went from Atlanta, Ga to Estonia last year and spent most of my time in the capital. I know it's very old, but it struck me as being heavily restored. I really enjoyed a day trip to Narva where we sat on the bank of the river dividing Estonia and Russia and listened to the propaganda being blared across the river by the Russians. We didn't understand it, but those in Narva do, as most of them speak Russian. Overall, it was a great visit and well worth the time and effort. We came via ferry from Helsinki. The lack of crowds was a refreshing change from the far more popular places in Europe. Do yourself a favor, and check it out in person.

replies.author: @dierstraits8399
replies.text:
​@Sudhir-n8f What is being broadcast on loudspeakers across the River, if it is not Russian propaganda, is it entertainment?  You don't say how learning the history of the region explains, in any way, that this is not propaganda? So there was a professor Stephen Cohen at Princeton, an alleged expert on USSR and Russia, so what; what was he saying that was relevant? There are proclaimed experts on every topic all over the World, how does Cohen change anything in regard to Estonia's situation? Estonia IS an independent country that IS currently worried about maintaining that independent democratic status; hardly surprising given the totally unjustified aggression and war crimes Estonia has watched Russia perpetrate on Ukraine. 

Please define your meaning of  Kaja Kalis "making real trouble in the EU in recent years", in what way? Kaja Kalis foresaw Russian aggression before the invasion of Ukraine, she warned of it and has done great work campaigning for European nations to combine their efforts to raise their resistance, how is that "trouble making?" Or do you want to see Russian control sweeping across Europe and enforcing communism in peaceful democratic nations? 

"Rubio and China refused to meet her" not a surprise, China has vested interests in supporting, at least tacitly, Russia. Perhaps she was lucky to avoid the kind of appalling and vile treatment the US administration gave to Volodymyr Zelenskyy on his visit to the White House. The USA's blackmail of an ally, to try to gain control of Ukraine's valuable resources, by intimidating Zelenskyy saying he "holds none of the cards." Well who is holding the cards now? Russia in retreat, their forces being wiped out in the thousands a week, Russian equipment ineffective (tanks, anti-aircraft radar and missile batteries bypassed and destroyed) by Ukrainian technologies, Russian fighter, tanker and radar aircraft being destroyed on the ground, at an increasing rate. 

Ukraine's home developed weaponry becoming ever more successful against Russia's illegal targeting of civilian target in cities, successful against cruise missiles and Iranian supplied drones. Ukraine now making drone strikes deep into Russia hitting oil and military targets and depleting their ability to fight? And the US now having to listen to Ukraine's expert advice on how to deal with Iranian armaments and fight with drones. 

Perhaps it is the US that needs to learn from history and look closely at how World War 2 played out? The war was prolonged by staying isolationist, until being forced into the war after being attacked and having profiteered for two years, while others fought to preserve their democratic freedom. Nato's treaty Article 5 is quite clear; so are you saying the USA will not support its allies, are you saying Estonia is expendable? Where does that end? What allied countries are not expendable? Don't forget the only Country to ever invoke Article 5 is the USA during the 9/11 attacks and allied nations provided support requested. The only country in history to go to war without being attacked, on a point of principle (the maintenance of democratic freedom of its allies), is the UK with the aid of its dominions.

replies.author: @Sudhir-n8f
replies.text: It is important to learn the history of the region before 'using terms' Russian propaganda. There was a professor at Princeton Stephen Cohen, a real expert on USSR and Russia. Estonia has become a real trouble maker in the EU in recent years. Kaja Kalis, EU's Foreign representative built her position on anti Russian chants. Rubio refused to meet her, China refused to talk to her. Estonians have been counting on the US support under Article 5 of the NATO. Last week Belgium debunked Article 5 protection. No US is not going to go to war for Estonia with Russia. Neither will Turkey. I spent an entire year in Baltics, and have spent 27 years in Europe after leaving the US.
